columns.CommandLine:
#
# This file MUST be edited with the 'visudo' command as root.
#
# Please consider adding local content in /etc/sudoers.d/ instead of
# directly modifying this file.

Defaults	env_reset

# This fixes CVE-2005-4890 and possibly b
columns.stdout_block:
Executing test: T1087.001-2 View sudoers access
#
# This file MUST be edited with the 'visudo' command as root.
#
# Please consider adding local content in /etc/sudoers.d/ instead of
# directly modifying this file.
#
# See the man page for details on how to write a sudoers file.
#
Defaults	env_reset
Defaults	mail_badpass
Defaults	secure_path="/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"
# This fixes CVE-2005-4890 and possibly breaks some versions of kdesu
# (#1011624, https://bugs.kde.org/s

sigma.title: Suspicious Network Connection to IP Lookup Service APIs

osquery.name: t1016_001_iface
osquery.query_text: SELECT interface, address, mask, type, friendly_name FROM interface_addresses WHERE interface != 'lo';

osquery.name: t1016_001_routes
osquery.query_text: SELECT destination, gateway, interface, type FROM routes WHERE destination != '::1' LIMIT 100;

story.narrative: Execution, telemetry, detections, artifacts, network observations, and SOAR response are tracked as separate witnesses and tied together through shared anchors.

promotion.reason: Full S5 validation (Linux Docker sandbox): 57 direct run(s), 1/8 Sigma rules matched with event linkage, 0 osquery correlations confirmed, raw telemetry preserved, analyst reviewed, clean baseline.

integrity.hash_method: SHA-256 of canonical JSON (sort_keys=True, default=str) excluding the integrity block itself
